zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Van Winkle Fox是Bertram Douglas Fox和Mrs Giles Fox的女兒，於1632年出生在英國越可爾波恩維其可。她於1670年3月10日與Thomas Anderson在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1684年在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁諾斯 福洛丁加姆享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁諾斯 福洛丁加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Van Winkle Fox是Bertram Douglas Fox和Mrs Giles Fox的女儿，于1632年出生在英国越可爾波恩維其可。她于1670年3月10日与Thomas Anderson在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1684年在英国英格兰越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁諾斯 福洛丁加姆享年52岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁諾斯 福洛丁加姆。

nameMeanings.name: Fox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from a word denoting the animal (Middle English, Old English fox), widely used to denote a sly or cunning individual. It was also used for someone with red hair. In England this surname absorbed some early examples of surnames derived from the ancient Germanic personal names mentioned at Faulks and Foulks .
Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an tSionnaigh ‘son of the fox’ (see Tinney ).
Irish: also adopted for Ó Catharnaigh, see Kearney .

variants.name: Foxe
meanings: Irish: variant of   Fox  .
Altered form of English   Fox  .

variants.name: Foxx
meanings: Variant of   Fox  .

variants.name: Faulks
meanings: English: from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Fauque, Fauques, Fau(l)ques (oblique case Fau(l)que), originally an ancient Germanic byname Falco, meaning ‘falcon’.

variants.name: Foxman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Charna, Isadore, Shmuel.
Jewish (American): variant of   Fox  .

variants.name: Fuchs
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Gerhard, Fritz, Mathias, Matthias, Armin, Ulrich, Erwin, Helmut.

German and Jewish: from Middle High German vuhs, German Fuchs ‘fox’, a nickname for a sly or cunning person, or for someone with red hair. This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic nicknames or surnames. Compare Fox 4, Fuks , and Futch .

variants.name: Kearney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Seamus, Caitlin, Declan, Dermot, Donovan, Eamonn, Liam.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Catharnaigh ‘descendant of Catharnach’, a personal name meaning ‘warlike’. Alternatively, it may be from Ó Cearnaigh or Mac Cearnaigh (from cearnach ‘victorious’) or from Ó Ceithearnaigh ‘descendant of Ceithearnach’, a personal name meaning ‘foot soldier’.

History: Most present-day bearers of this name are descended from Tadhg Ó Catharnaigh, killed in battle in 1084. He was nicknamed An Sionnach ‘the Fox’, and for this reason the Irish surname was sometimes Anglicized as Fox . Denis Kearney, born in Oakmont, Ireland, in 1847, was a US labor movement leader.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Ivan   or   Evan  , as in the case of the American film actor  Van Heflin  (  1910–71 , born  Emmett Evan Heflin ) and the Northern Irish singer  Van Morrison  (  b. 1945  as  George Ivan Morrison ).

nameMeanings.name: Winkle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Winkel  .

English (Staffordshire): habitational name from Wincle (Cheshire) or possibly from Winkhill in Waterfall (Staffordshire). The placenames probably derive from Old English wincel ‘nook, corner’, though the Cheshire placename may alternatively derive from Old English wince ‘sharp bend in a river, corner’ + hyll ‘hill’.

variants.name: Winkles
meanings: English: variant of   Winkle  .
Americanized form of German   Winkels  .

variants.name: Winkel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Arno, Gerhardt, Aloysius, Bernd, Erwin, Gerd, Horst, Kurt, Math, Philo, Theodor.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived on a piece of land enclosed by woods or rivers or on a street corner in a town or city, from Middle High German winkel, German Winkel ‘corner’. In time this word also came to denote a corner shop (see Winkelmann , Winkler ). This surname is also found in Denmark, Poland, and France (mainly Lorraine).
German: habitational name from any of various places called Winkel.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Van Winkle Fox was born in 1632, in Bonwick, Yorkshire, England as the daughter of Bertram Douglas Fox and Mrs Giles Fox. She married Thomas Anderson on 10 March 1670, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1684, in North Frodingham, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in North Frodingham, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Thomas Anderson le 10 mars 1670, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1684, à North Frodingham, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à North Frodingham, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Van Winkle Foxは1632年にイギリスヨークシュアイアボンウィックでBertram Douglas FoxとMrs Giles Foxの娘として生まれた。1670年3月10日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアでThomas Andersonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1684年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングノース フロディンガムで52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングノース フロディンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Van Winkle Fox(은)는 1632년 영국 요르크실르 보윅에서 Bertram Douglas Fox(와)과 Mrs Giles Fox의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1670년 3월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Thomas Anderson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1684년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 노르스 프로딩함에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 노르스 프로딩함에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Van Winkle Fox родилась в 1632 г. в местности бонвикк, еркшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Bertram Douglas Fox и Mrs Giles Fox. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Anderson 10 марта 1670 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1684 г. в местности норт фродингхам, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности норт фродингхам, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания.
